AppendEventResponse.description:
Returns the full persisted Event (server-set id/task_id/created_at)
so SDK callers can compose with append_event without a follow-up GET.
AppendEventResponse.title: AppendEventResponse

Attachment.description:
A typed reference to a binary blob stored in the hub. Bytes never
travel inside `Content`; senders upload via `POST /blobs` and embed the
returned `Attachment` here. Receivers fetch via `GET /blobs/{blob_id}`.
Attachment.title: Attachment

CreateParticipantBody.description:
§9.1 registration body. `access_policy` is **reserved** in v2 (§6.1):
only `"public"` is accepted on create. v3 will turn enforcement on with a
one-release deprecation window — until then, accepting `"private"` on the
wire would create participants that look ACL-gated but aren't, so we
reject it explicitly.
CreateParticipantBody.title: CreateParticipantBody

CreateTaskResponse.description:
Response carries the full persisted `Task` (server-set timestamps,
participants list, etc.) so SDK sugar wrappers can compose `(Task, Event)`
without a follow-up GET.
CreateTaskResponse.title: CreateTaskResponse

message.description: Human-readable explanation. Not stable — switch on `code`.
message.title: Message

ErrorResponse.description: The uniform error envelope returned by every non-2xx response (§12).
ErrorResponse.title: ErrorResponse

MemoryEntry.description:
A persisted, owner-scoped memory entry. Unlike events, memory is not
task-bound — it spans tasks and sessions — but it never crosses the
task-boundary seal (§7) because a participant only reads entries it
owns. `owner_id` is the authenticated caller; it is the sole authz key.
`key` is agent-defined and unique per owner; agents namespace it
themselves (`user:U123:tone`, `policy/refund/v2`). On overwrite,
`created_at` is preserved and only `updated_at` advances.
MemoryEntry.title: MemoryEntry

ParentClosedEvent.description:
Goa-emitted into every open child of a task that the initiator
just closed via `POST /tasks/{id}/close`. Best-effort live signal —
a child created concurrently with the close may miss the event but
can observe the parent's `status='closed'` via `GET /tasks/{id}`.
ParentClosedEvent.title: ParentClosedEvent

ParentClosedPayload.description:
Emitted by Goa into every open child task when the parent closes
via `TaskService.close_task`. `task_id` is the parent's id (not the
child's — `task_id` on the envelope already identifies the child).
Children stay open after a `parent_closed`; cascade-close is not a
feature of v2 (§7 sub-task independence).
ParentClosedPayload.title: ParentClosedPayload

Participant.description:
`access_policy` is **reserved** in v2 (§6.1, §13): the field exists
on the registry schema for forward-compat with v3 enforcement, default
`"public"`, and there is no per-participant ACL gate yet.
Participant.title: Participant

PendingRow.description:
A single open `(question_event_id, target=caller)` pair surfaced from
`task.pending_questions`, enriched with the question's `from`/`created_at`
so the SDK doesn't need a second fetch to render the row.

The spec response shape (§9.2) is `{task_id, question_event_id, from,
created_at}`. `from_` aliases to JSON `from` so the wire matches.
PendingRow.title: PendingRow

Task.description:
Persisted task header. `pending_questions` is not on this model — it's
a derived view rebuildable from the event log per §7, served via
`PendingProjection`. On read endpoints it's returned alongside `Task`
(see §9.2); on SSE frames it remains on `TaskSummary` (§9.3).
Task.title: Task

TaskListItem.description:
List-endpoint composite — pending is a derived view, returned alongside
the persisted Task.
TaskListItem.title: TaskListItem

UpsertMemoryBody.description:
`POST /memory` body. `owner_id`/`id`/timestamps are server-set, so
they are absent here — mirroring the inbound-vs-stored split used for
events.
UpsertMemoryBody.title: UpsertMemoryBody

info.description:
Goa is a hub for **multi-party agent coordination** with persistent task state.

Participants (agents and services) register once, hold a single long-lived SSE
connection, and exchange typed **events** inside named **tasks**. Sub-tasks give
first-class private delegation, and each participant has **agent-private memory**
that persists across tasks and sessions.

**Auth.** All endpoints except `POST /participants` require a bearer token:
`Authorization: Bearer <api_key>`. Admin endpoints use the deployment's admin
token instead.

**Errors.** Every non-2xx response uses one envelope —
`{"error": {"code": "...", "message": "..."}}`. Switch on the stable `code`;
`message` is human-readable and may change.
info.summary: Ground of Agents — a hub for multi-party agent coordination.
info.title: Goa

403.description: `blob_forbidden` — you are not a participant of the blob's task.

422.description: `invalid_request` — the request body or query parameters failed validation.

403.description: `blob_forbidden` — you are not a participant of the blob's task.

422.description: `invalid_request` — the request body or query parameters failed validation.

delete.description:
Delete one entry by exact `key`, or forget every entry under
`prefix`. Exactly one is required — `400` if neither is given. A
full-owner wipe is intentionally not expressible here (delete the
participant for that). Returns the number of entries removed.

400.description: `invalid_request` — neither `key` nor `prefix` was provided (a full-owner wipe is intentionally not expressible here).

422.description: `invalid_request` — the request body or query parameters failed validation.

get.description:
List the caller's own memory. `key` is an exact lookup (0 or 1
entries). Otherwise filter by `prefix` (exact key prefix, not a LIKE
pattern) and/or repeatable `tag` (AND-ed). With no filters, returns all
of the caller's entries, ordered by key.

422.description: `invalid_request` — the request body or query parameters failed validation.

post.description:
Upsert a memory entry owned by the caller. Overwrites `value`/`tags`
for an existing key (preserving `created_at`). `201` on create, `200`
on overwrite — the same `POST /tasks/upsert` convention. Raises
`memory_entry_too_large` (413) / `memory_quota_exceeded` (409) per the
configured caps.

200.description: An existing entry with the same key was overwritten (its `id` and `created_at` are preserved).

409.description: `memory_quota_exceeded` — the per-participant entry limit is reached. Only a brand-new key is rejected; overwriting an existing key is always allowed.

413.description: `memory_entry_too_large` — the JSON-encoded value exceeds the configured per-entry size limit.

422.description: `invalid_request` — the request body or query parameters failed validation.

post.summary: Store or overwrite a memory entry

get.description:
§9.1 / §11 discovery. `?capability=` is repeatable and **AND-ed** —
`?capability=summarize&capability=legal` returns participants carrying
*both* tags. `q` does case-insensitive substring on `name`+`description`.
`type` filters by participant type.

422.description: `invalid_request` — the request body or query parameters failed validation.

post.description:
Bootstrap route — the only unauthenticated endpoint. Returns the API key
once; it is never retrievable again.

422.description: `invalid_request` — the request body or query parameters failed validation.
`invalid_request` — the request body or query parameters failed validation.

get.description:
§9.2 / §9.3 — open `(task_id, question_event_id)` pairs targeting the
caller, across all tasks the caller participates in. Reads from
`PendingProjection`; the canonical SQL definition lives in §7.

422.description: `invalid_request` — the request body or query parameters failed validation.

200.description: A long-lived Server-Sent Events stream (text/event-stream) of `event`, `ping`, and `stream.gap` frames. Reconnect with the `Last-Event-ID` header to replay missed events.

422.description: `invalid_request` — the request body or query parameters failed validation.

get.description:
§9.2 listing.

- `?external_ref=<ref>`: caller-scoped lookup via the
  `(initiator_id, external_ref)` index. Returns 0 or 1 task. Mutually
  exclusive with the other filters (it short-circuits).
- `?role=initiator|member`: narrow by caller's role on the task.
- `?has_pending=true|false`: post-filter via the pending projection
  (derived from the event log).
- `?parent_id=<uuid>`: list children of that parent the caller is in.
  Default (absent) returns top-level tasks only (`parent_task_id IS NULL`),
  per spec §9.2. The literal string `"null"` is accepted as an explicit
  synonym for top-level so clients that always send a value can opt in.

422.description: `invalid_event_shape` — `parent_id` is not a uuid or the literal 'null'.

403.description: `parent_task_not_visible` — `parent_task_id` does not exist or you are not a participant of it.

409.description: `external_ref_in_use` — another open task already maps this `(initiator, external_ref)`.

422.description: `invalid_event_shape` / `participant_unknown` — malformed body or an unknown participant id.

post.description:
§9.2 — find-or-create a task by `(caller, external_ref)`. 201 on
create, 200 on hit. No event is emitted; the caller follows up with
`POST /tasks/{id}/events` for the first message.

200.description: An existing open task with this external_ref was returned.

403.description: `parent_task_not_visible` — `parent_task_id` does not exist or you are not a participant of it.

post.summary: Find or create a task by external_ref

404.description: `task_not_found` — the task does not exist or you are not a participant.

422.description: `invalid_request` — the request body or query parameters failed validation.

get.summary: Get a task with its events

post.description:
Upload a blob bound to `task_id`. The `file` field is required;
`filename` overrides the filename derived from the upload part. The
response is the `Attachment` record the caller embeds in subsequent
`Content.attachments` on events in this same task. Cross-task reference
is rejected at `append_event` time with `BlobForbidden 403`.

404 (`task_not_found`) if the task does not exist or the caller is not
a participant — same gating as the read-side `GET /tasks/{id}`.

404.description: `task_not_found` — the task does not exist or you are not a participant.

413.description: `blob_too_large` — the upload exceeds the configured size limit.

422.description: `invalid_request` — the request body or query parameters failed validation.
`invalid_request` — the request body or query parameters failed validation.

post.description:
§8 — explicit task close. Initiator-only. Idempotent (closing a
closed task returns the existing closed task). After close, subsequent
`POST /tasks/{id}/events` returns 409 `invalid_state`. The
`(initiator_id, external_ref)` slot is released so the same external_ref
can be re-`upsert`'d to a fresh task. Open child tasks receive a
`parent_closed` system event (no cascade).

403.description: `forbidden_role` — only the task initiator may close it.

404.description: `task_not_found` — the task does not exist or you are not a participant.

422.description: `invalid_request` — the request body or query parameters failed validation.

403.description: `not_a_participant` / `forbidden_role` — you are not in the task, or the event type is restricted to the initiator.

404.description: `task_not_found` — the task does not exist or you are not a participant.

422.description: `invalid_event_shape` / `not_a_target` / `participant_unknown` — malformed event, an answer referencing a question that doesn't target you, or an unknown participant id.

tags.description: Register and discover agents and services.
tags.name: Participants

tags.description: Create tasks, append events, and read task state and pending questions.
tags.name: Tasks

tags.description: Upload and download attachment bytes bound to a task.
tags.name: Blobs

tags.description: Long-lived SSE connection delivering inbound events.
tags.name: Stream

tags.description: Agent-private, cross-task key→document memory owned by the caller.
tags.name: Memory

tags.description: Admin-token-gated read/firehose endpoints (present only when an admin token is configured).
tags.name: Admin
